  "text": "The SASL mechanism configuration validation at broker startup in Apache Kafka spans several Java (and Scala) source files:\n\n**1. sasl.enabled.mechanisms validation**\n\n- `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`: The `saslEnabledMechanisms()` private method reads the enabled mechanisms per listener name. The `validate()` block asserts that `sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol` is contained in `sasl.enabled.mechanisms` when SASL is used for inter-broker communication.\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/ChannelBuilders.java`: In `serverChannelBuilder()` and the private `create()` method, the server-side code iterates over each mechanism in `BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` and calls `JaasContext.loadServerContext()` for each one, triggering JAAS presence validation.\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java`: In its constructor (lines 177-186), it validates that `sasl.enabled.mechanisms` is non-null and non-empty, and that a callback handler and Subject exist for every listed mechanism.\n\n**2. JAAS config presence check**\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/JaasContext.java`: The `loadServerContext()` method loads or locates the JAAS configuration for each enabled mechanism. `defaultContext()` throws `IllegalArgumentException` with a descriptive message if neither the listener-scoped context name (e.g., `sasl_plaintext.KafkaServer`) nor the global `KafkaServer` entry is found in the JAAS configuration file. The `load()` method validates that the dynamic `sasl.jaas.config` property contains exactly one login module.\n\n**3. Mechanism-specific configuration validation**\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/SaslChannelBuilder.java`: `createServerCallbackHandlers()` (lines 317-335) selects the appropriate callback handler per mechanism:\n  - PLAIN → `PlainServerCallbackHandler`\n  - SCRAM-SHA-256 / SCRAM-SHA-512 → `ScramServerCallbackHandler` (requires `credentialCache`)\n  - GSSAPI → falls back to `SaslServerCallbackHandler`; also triggers `KerberosLogin` via `defaultLoginClass()` and adds native GSS credentials\n  - OAUTHBEARER → `OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler` (default) or a custom class\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramServerCallbackHandler.java`: Handles SCRAM credential lookup from the broker credential cache.\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/OAuthBearerValidatorCallbackHandler.java`: The production OAUTHBEARER validator; `configure()` validates JWKS endpoint URL and JWT validator configuration.\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/unsecured/OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler.java`: The default (non-production) OAUTHBEARER validator; its `configure()` checks that exactly one non-null JAAS mechanism entry is provided.\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/OAuthBearerLoginCallbackHandler.java`: Validates OAUTHBEARER login configuration including token endpoint URL.\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/CommonClientConfigs.java`: `postValidateSaslMechanismConfig()` ensures that when `security.protocol` is `SASL_PLAINTEXT` or `SASL_SSL`, the `sasl.mechanism` is non-null and non-empty.\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java`: Defines `SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG` and related broker security configuration constants used throughout the validation chain."
}

## SASL Mechanism Configuration Validation at Broker Startup

The validation is distributed across several files:

### 1. `sasl.enabled.mechanisms` Validation
- **`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`** — `saslEnabledMechanisms()` reads enabled mechanisms; `validate()` requires that `sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol` is in `sasl.enabled.mechanisms`
- **`clients/.../network/ChannelBuilders.java`** — `serverChannelBuilder()` iterates over all enabled mechanisms and calls `JaasContext.loadServerContext()` for each
- **`clients/.../authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java`** — constructor validates that enabled mechanisms list is non-empty, and that a callback handler and Subject exist for every mechanism

### 2. JAAS Config Presence Check
- **`clients/.../security/JaasContext.java`** — `loadServerContext()` / `defaultContext()` / `load()`: throws `IllegalArgumentException` if no JAAS entry is found for a mechanism (either via `sasl.jaas.config` property or the static JAAS file under `KafkaServer` / `<listener>.KafkaServer` context names)

### 3. Mechanism-Specific Configuration Validation
- **`clients/.../network/SaslChannelBuilder.java`** — `createServerCallbackHandlers()` dispatches to the correct handler per mechanism (SCRAM → `ScramServerCallbackHandler`, OAUTHBEARER → `OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler`, GSSAPI → `KerberosLogin`)
- **`clients/.../scram/internals/ScramServerCallbackHandler.java`** — SCRAM handler backed by credential cache
- **`clients/.../oauthbearer/OAuthBearerValidatorCallbackHandler.java`** — Production OAUTHBEARER validator (JWKS endpoint, JWT validator)
- **`clients/.../oauthbearer/internals/unsecured/OAuthBearerUnsecuredValidatorCallbackHandler.java`** — Default OAUTHBEARER validator (validates exactly one JAAS entry)
- **`clients/.../oauthbearer/OAuthBearerLoginCallbackHandler.java`** — Validates OAUTHBEARER login config (token endpoint URL)
